summary: Add OpenClaw/Polaris edit marker to footer
details: Small HTML comment in index.html footer to verify end-to-end editing and deployment from Raspberry Pi via OpenClaw.

summary: Create Polaris public and private dashboards
details: Added public Polaris activity dashboard at polaris.html and initial private dashboard shell at /private/index.html for Cloudflare Access gating.

summary: Redesign public Polaris dashboard with grouped, collapsible log
details: Updated polaris.html to add overview cards, per-day collapsible panels, and light animations, plus dedicated CSS in assets/css/polaris.css.

summary: Add Polaris TODO section to private dashboard
details: Extended /private/index.html with a visible Polaris TODO list summarizing pending work like wiring live Pi status, finishing Cloudflare Access, and future dashboard ideas.

summary: Add README documenting portfolio + Polaris architecture
details: Created README.md explaining that this repo backs devrobotics.dev, is managed by an OpenClaw agent (Polaris) on a Raspberry Pi, and includes a high-level system design + process flow for changes.

summary: Add disclaimer noting README is maintained by Polaris
details: Appended a disclaimer to README.md with a warning icon explaining that the README was drafted and is maintained by the Polaris agent running on the Raspberry Pi via OpenClaw.

summary: Fix clipping on long Polaris log entries
details: Increased the max-height on open day panels in assets/css/polaris.css so long descriptions are no longer visually cut off at the bottom of the card.

summary: Add sidebar link from portfolio to Polaris dashboard
details: Updated index.html sidebar navigation to include a direct link to /polaris.html so it feels like a first-class part of the site.

summary: Add scrollable bodies to daily Polaris log panels
details: Changed the open state of polaris-day-body to use a fixed max-height with overflow-y:auto so long day logs can be scrolled inside the card instead of being visually clipped.

summary: Revert main sidebar to original layout
details: Restored index.html sidebar navigation to the original five-section layout (Introduction, Current Research, Featured Projects, At a Glance, Contact) and removed the Polaris Dashboard link to keep the portfolio section visually unchanged.

summary: Add global header with theme toggle and logo
details: Introduced a persistent site-header on index.html, polaris.html, and main project pages with a left-aligned light/dark theme toggle and right-aligned logo (images/icon.png), wired to a small script that remembers the chosen theme via localStorage.

summary: Switch header background to solid black for visibility
details: Updated .site-header to use an opaque black background and slightly larger top padding so the new global header bar stands out clearly above the dark page background.

summary: Refine devrobotics site layout, theming, and Polaris integration
details: Converted the main site to a light/dark theme with a global top nav, updated the hero to reflect Hardik's role at Amazon Robotics, rewrote the graduate UAV research section into concise bullet points, aligned Polaris with the new layout (including a Powered by Polaris & OpenClaw section), and applied consistent header + theme behavior across project detail pages.

summary: Adjust Polaris change log panel height
details: Tuned the CSS for .polaris-day-body.open to use viewport-based min/max heights so each change log day opens to a larger, more readable box (~30–70% of the viewport) while still allowing scrolling for very long days.

summary: Highlight Polaris assistant in hero text
details: Updated the main hero section to mention Polaris as a personal assistant running on a Raspberry Pi using OpenClaw, helping maintain the site and automate workflows.

summary: Default site theme to dark mode
details: Changed the theme initialization logic so that dark mode is the default when no preference is stored, while still respecting light/dark choices via localStorage across the home, Polaris, and project pages.

summary: Format Polaris change log dates with full day and time
details: Updated polaris.html to format change log dates as full strings like 'Saturday, March 14th 2026 | 12:34 PM' both in the Latest Activity panel and in each entry's metadata.

summary: Wire Raspberry Pi status into Polaris dashboard
details: Added an auto-generated JSON snapshot (polaris-pi-status.json) with Pi metrics like CPU temperature, load, disk usage, and OpenClaw gateway status, plus a Python script (update_pi_status.py) that refreshes the JSON and pushes changes to GitHub. Updated polaris.html to read this static JSON so the public Polaris dashboard shows live-ish Pi status.

summary: Tidy Pi status display and add pulsing online indicator
details: Refined the public Polaris dashboard Pi status card to use a pulsing green dot next to the Online label, separate lines for host/CPU/load/disk/OpenClaw/updated fields, and a human-friendly formatted timestamp instead of the raw ISO string. Also tightened the OpenClaw status summary in the JSON to avoid exposing IPs or other sensitive details.

summary: Make Pi status indicator more visible
details: Increased the size of the Pi status dot, added a white border and stronger glow so the pulsing Online indicator stands out clearly against the dark card background.

summary: Add RAM usage to public Pi status dashboard
details: Extended the Pi status JSON and Polaris dashboard to include RAM usage (used/total) alongside CPU temperature, load, and disk usage. The Pi-side script now parses `free -h` into a friendly RAM string and the dashboard renders it on its own line.

summary: Simplify Pi status dot styling for remote site
details: Removed the inline bullet character and white border from the Pi status dot, leaving a single glowing green circle rendered via CSS so that both localhost and the deployed GitHub Pages site use the same simple, cache-friendly styling.
